name: Phat Thai (Pad Thai)
recipeIngredient: 4 Slender green onions, sliced into 1-inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak rice noodles in warm water to cover for 15 to 20 minutes. Prepare all remaining ingredients and place them next to the stove, along with a small serving platter.
2. When the noodles are very limp and white, drain and measure out 2-1/2 cups. Set aside near the stove as well.
3. Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat and add 2 tbsp oil, swirling to coat the surface.
4. Add garlic and toss until golden, about 30 seconds.
5. Add shrimp (and optional pork or beef) and cook until pink for shrimp, or light brown if using meat, for no more than 1 minute.
6. Remove shrimp from the pan and set aside.
7. Add the egg to the pan and tilt to spread into a thin sheet. Scramble until it breaks into small lumps; remove and set aside with the shrimp.
8. Heat another 2 tbsp oil in the wok or skillet over medium-high heat for 30 seconds, then add the noodles. Cook the noodles until they soften and curl into ivory ringlets, gently turning them to coat the pan evenly.
9. Add fish sauce, sugar, and peanuts; continue to turn the noodles to season.
10. Reserve some bean sprouts and sliced green onions for garnish. Add the rest of the beansprouts, green onions, shrimp, and egg mixture.
11. Cook for 1 minute, turning often, until fully combined.
12. Transfer noodles to the serving platter and squeeze lime wedges over the top. Garnish with remaining bean sprouts and serve immediately. Serves 1 very hungry person or 2 appetizers.
